items.question: Which of the following is NOT a property of a unit test?
options: exercises one small unit in isolation
items.explanation: Hitting the real DB is integration, not unit. Mock the DB in unit tests.

items.question: The AAA pattern stands for:
items.explanation: The three phases of a well-structured unit test.

items.question: How do you assert that a function raises an exception in pytest?
items.explanation: Bare try/except silently passes if no exception is raised; pytest.raises fails the test in that case.

items.question: What does 100% line coverage guarantee?
options: every line was executed by some test (says nothing about asserts)
items.answer: every line was executed by some test (says nothing about asserts)
items.explanation: Coverage measures execution, not assertion quality.

items.question: Which is the right tool for running the same test logic with many inputs?
items.explanation: Each parameter set becomes a separately reported test case.

items.question: A test that passes locally but fails randomly in CI is called:
items.explanation: Flakiness usually comes from shared mutable state, real time, networks or unordered iteration.
